title: Hymenophyllum preslii
intro: Hymenophyllum preslii, also known as Preslie's fern or Maori's feather fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hymenophyllum preslii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Maori medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, while in modern herbalism, it is also used to make capsules, tinctures, and infusions.
taxonomy: Hymenophyllum preslii is classified as a species of fern within the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy fern family. This family is further classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and fern allies. The order is Hymenophyllales, and the genus Hymenophyllum is a part of this order. Hymenophyllum preslii is a member of the family Hymenophyllaceae, characterized by its delicate and filmy fronds. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions, with a wide distribution in the Old World.
